title: Dinner at a friend's house
setting: You've been invited to Roberto's home for dinner. His mother greets you at the door.

steps.speaker: Doña Rosa
steps.en: Come in, come in! So glad you came.

options.en: Thank you for inviting me, ma'am.
options.feedback: Gracious and respectful — 'doña' is just the right touch of warmth.
Too casual for a host welcoming you in. A thank-you sets the right tone.
Ha! Playful and charming — Nica hosts love a guest ready to eat.

steps.speaker: Doña Rosa
steps.es: Siéntese. Hice gallo pinto y carne. Coma, coma.
steps.en: Sit down. I made gallo pinto and meat. Eat, eat.

options.en: It looks delicious. Thank you, ma'am.
options.feedback: Complimenting the food before eating delights any cook. Perfect.
Ouch — she cooked for you! That would hurt her feelings here.
Polite, though she'll likely insist on serving you a generous plate herself.

steps.speaker: Doña Rosa
steps.en: Did you like it? Want more?

options.es: Estaba delicioso. Un poquito más, gracias.
options.en: It was delicious. A little more, thanks.
options.feedback: Accepting seconds is a compliment in itself. 'Estaba delicioso' is perfect past tense.

options.es: Sí, todo muy rico. Ya quedé lleno.
options.en: Yes, all delicious. I'm full now.
options.feedback: Gracious either way — praising the food while gently declining more is smooth.
'Just okay' about a home-cooked meal? That stings. Always praise the cook.

steps.speaker: Doña Rosa
steps.es: Aquí siempre tiene su plato, mijo.
steps.en: Here you always have your plate, dear.

options.es: Eso significa mucho para mí. Gracias.
options.en: That means a lot to me. Thank you.
options.feedback: Heartfelt — she just treated you like family, and you honored it.
Warm and respectful. A lovely way to receive her generosity.
Way too abrupt after such a tender thing to say! Meet warmth with warmth.
